Complex analysis — amplitwist, contours, residues

A Needham-flavored tour: holomorphic maps as local rotations, Cauchy's theorem as a curl-free vector field, residues as bookkeeping for poles, and conformal maps as the geometry of $\hat{\mathbb{C}}$.

blocks.html:
1. The plane and the sphere

The complex numbers $\mathbb{C}$ are $\mathbb{R}^2$ with multiplication added. A complex number $z=x+iy = re^{i\theta}$ is a point with a length and an angle. The metric is the Euclidean one: $|z-w| = \sqrt{(x_1-x_2)^2+(y_1-y_2)^2}$.

For complex analysis the right ambient space is not $\mathbb{C}$ but its one-point compactification, the Riemann sphere $\hat{\mathbb{C}} = \mathbb{C}\cup\{\infty\}$. Place the sphere of radius $\tfrac12$ tangent to the plane at the origin and project from the north pole; this is stereographic projection. The plane corresponds to the sphere minus the north pole, and the formal symbol $\infty$ is the north pole itself.

Two facts make stereographic projection the natural picture:

conformal it preserves angles, and circles ↔ circles it sends circles on the sphere to circles or lines in the plane (a line being a "circle through $\infty$").

A Möbius transformation is any map $T(z) = \dfrac{az+b}{cz+d}$ with $ad-bc\ne 0$. Three remarkable facts:

(i) Möbius transformations form a group, isomorphic to $\mathrm{PGL}_2(\mathbb{C})$.
(ii) On $\hat{\mathbb{C}}$ they are precisely the conformal automorphisms — bijective angle-preserving maps.
(iii) On the sphere they are exactly the rigid rotations and reflections combined with dilations — but if you restrict to the subgroup that preserves the spherical metric, you recover $\mathrm{SO}(3)$ acting as the rotation group of the sphere, realized in coordinates as $T(z) = \dfrac{az - \bar c}{cz + \bar a}$ with $|a|^2+|c|^2=1$ (i.e.\ $\mathrm{SU}(2)/\{\pm 1\}$).

A Möbius transformation is determined by what it does to three points: given any two triples of distinct points $(z_1,z_2,z_3)$ and $(w_1,w_2,w_3)$ in $\hat{\mathbb{C}}$, there is a unique $T$ with $T(z_i)=w_i$. The "three points" are the analogue of "two points determine a line" in projective geometry.

The invariant of four points under all Möbius transformations is the cross-ratio

$$[z_1,z_2;z_3,z_4] \;=\; \frac{(z_1-z_3)(z_2-z_4)}{(z_1-z_4)(z_2-z_3)}.$$

Cross-ratio is real iff the four points are concyclic — a tidy way to detect "four points on a circle" using only complex arithmetic.

2. Holomorphic functions — the amplitwist picture

A function $f:U\to\mathbb{C}$ on an open set $U\subseteq\mathbb{C}$ is holomorphic at $z_0$ if

$$f'(z_0) \;=\; \lim_{h\to 0}\frac{f(z_0+h)-f(z_0)}{h}$$

exists as a complex limit — meaning the limit is the same no matter how $h\in\mathbb{C}$ approaches $0$. This is much more restrictive than $\mathbb{R}^2$-differentiability.

Write $f = u+iv$, viewed as a map $\mathbb{R}^2\to\mathbb{R}^2$. Real-differentiability gives a Jacobian

$$Df = \begin{pmatrix} u_x & u_y \\ v_x & v_y \end{pmatrix}.$$

For $f$ to be complex-differentiable, this Jacobian must commute with multiplication by $i$ — i.e.\ it must be a similarity matrix (a rotation composed with a scaling). A short calculation shows this is equivalent to the Cauchy–Riemann equations

$$u_x = v_y, \qquad u_y = -v_x,$$

and then $f'(z_0) = u_x + i v_x = v_y - i u_y$.

Amplitwist. Needham's word: near $z_0$, a holomorphic map acts on infinitesimal vectors as rotation by $\arg f'(z_0)$ and scaling by $|f'(z_0)|$. That single complex number $f'(z_0)$ encodes both. Wherever $f'(z_0)\ne 0$, $f$ is conformal: it preserves angles between curves through $z_0$.

Compare: the map $z\mapsto \bar z$ is real-differentiable but not holomorphic. Its Jacobian is a reflection, not a rotation+scaling. Reflections reverse orientation, so $\bar z$ is "anti-conformal" — it preserves angle magnitudes but flips signs.

The amplitwist picture is what makes complex analysis qualitatively different from $\mathbb{R}^2$ analysis. Every theorem we are about to see is, in some sense, a topological consequence of "all infinitesimal motion is rotation + scaling."

params.title: Amplitwist visualizer
params.hint: click on the left to choose $z_0$

blocks.html:
The widget above shows a small grid of points $z_0 + \varepsilon\{(\pm 1,\pm 1)\}$ on the left, and $f$ of those points on the right. When $f$ is holomorphic, the right grid is a rotated, scaled, possibly translated copy of the left — angles preserved. For $\overline{z}$, the right grid is reflected.

Domain coloring

A picture of a complex function $f:\mathbb{C}\to\mathbb{C}$ requires four real dimensions. The standard 2D summary: encode $\arg f(z)$ as hue, $|f(z)|$ as lightness, and plot over the $z$-plane. Zeros become darkness sinks, poles become bright cones, and a winding rainbow around any point reveals its order — $n$ rainbows for a zero of order $n$, $n$ rainbows the other way for a pole of order $n$.

layout.html: 2.5
Vertical axis points up (mathematical convention). The hue wheel marks: red ↔ +1, yellow ↔ $e^{i\pi/3}$, green ↔ $i$, cyan ↔ −1, blue ↔ −i.

3. Contour integration

A contour is a piecewise smooth curve $\gamma:[a,b]\to\mathbb{C}$. Given a continuous $f$ on its image, define

$$\int_\gamma f(z)\,dz \;=\; \int_a^b f(\gamma(t))\,\gamma'(t)\,dt.$$

Read $dz = dx + i\,dy$ as a complex-valued 1-form. Then $f\,dz = (u+iv)(dx+i\,dy) = (u\,dx - v\,dy) + i(v\,dx + u\,dy)$, two real line integrals.

The key observation: those two real 1-forms are closed exactly when $u,v$ satisfy Cauchy–Riemann. The "real part" 1-form $u\,dx - v\,dy$ has $d = (-v_x - u_y)\,dx\wedge dy$, which vanishes iff $u_y = -v_x$. The "imaginary part" 1-form $v\,dx + u\,dy$ has $d = (u_x - v_y)\,dx\wedge dy$, vanishing iff $u_x = v_y$. Both pieces are closed precisely when $f$ is holomorphic.

Curl-free, source-free. Think of $\overline{f(z)}$ as a vector field on the plane. Cauchy–Riemann equations say its divergence and curl both vanish: it is incompressible and irrotational. That is the physical content of "$f$ is holomorphic," and it is why integrals around closed loops vanish.

Cauchy's theorem (star-shaped form). If $f$ is holomorphic on a star-shaped open set $U$ and $\gamma\subset U$ is a closed contour, then $\oint_\gamma f\,dz = 0$.

Sketch: holomorphic $f$ on a star-shaped (in particular simply connected) domain has an antiderivative $F$ — define $F(z) = \int_{z_*}^z f$ along straight rays from the star centre. Then $\oint f\,dz = F(\text{end}) - F(\text{start}) = 0$ for a closed loop. Equivalently: by Stokes, $\oint_\gamma f\,dz = \iint_D df\wedge dz$, but $df = f'\,dz$ so $df\wedge dz = 0$.

The general (Goursat) version drops the $C^1$ hypothesis on $f'$ and works for any simply connected $U$. The proof bisects triangles and is elegant; we won't reproduce it here.

Cauchy's integral formula

If $f$ is holomorphic on a domain containing the closed disk $\overline{D(z_0,r)}$, then for every interior point $a\in D(z_0,r)$,

$$f(a) \;=\; \frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_{|z-z_0|=r}\frac{f(z)}{z-a}\,dz.$$

The values of $f$ on the boundary determine $f$ in the interior — a phenomenon with no analogue in real analysis. Differentiating under the integral $n$ times,

$$f^{(n)}(a) \;=\; \frac{n!}{2\pi i}\oint \frac{f(z)}{(z-a)^{n+1}}\,dz.$$

Holomorphic functions are infinitely differentiable. That is the main miracle.

4. Consequences

From Cauchy's integral formula nearly everything follows.

Power series everywhere

If $f$ is holomorphic on $D(z_0,R)$, then for any $a\in D(z_0,R)$, the geometric series in $1/(z-a) = 1/((z-z_0) - (a-z_0))$ converges uniformly on $|z-z_0|=r$ for $|a-z_0|

$$f(a) = \sum_{n=0}^\infty c_n (a-z_0)^n, \qquad c_n = \frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint \frac{f(z)}{(z-z_0)^{n+1}}dz = \frac{f^{(n)}(z_0)}{n!}.$$

Holomorphic ⟺ analytic, with radius of convergence equal to the distance from $z_0$ to the nearest singularity.

Liouville and FTA

Liouville's theorem. A bounded entire function is constant. Proof: by CIF for $f'$, $|f'(z_0)| \le M/r$ for any $r$, so $f'\equiv 0$.

Fundamental theorem of algebra. Every nonconstant polynomial $p(z)$ has a complex root. Proof 1: if $p$ never vanishes, $1/p$ is bounded entire (it tends to $0$ at infinity), hence constant by Liouville — absurd.

The geometric proof of FTA. Consider a circle of huge radius $R$. On it, $p(z)\sim a_n z^n$, so $p$ wraps $|z|=R$ around $0$ exactly $n$ times. Now shrink the circle to a point. At radius $0$, $p$ is constant $p(0)$, wrapping $0$ times. Winding number is integer-valued and varies continuously, so somewhere along the way it must cross zero — and that means $p$ hits $0$ at some intermediate $z$.

Morera, max modulus, open mapping, Schwarz

Morera. If $f$ is continuous on $U$ and $\oint_\Delta f = 0$ for every triangle $\Delta\subset U$, then $f$ is holomorphic. (Converse to Cauchy.)

Maximum modulus. If $f$ is holomorphic on $U$ and $|f|$ attains a local max at an interior point, $f$ is constant. (Hence on a compact domain, $|f|$ achieves its sup on the boundary.)

Open mapping. A nonconstant holomorphic map sends open sets to open sets. (One way to see it: $f-w_0$ has finitely many zeros, none on a small circle, and Rouché shows $f-w$ has the same number for all $w$ near $w_0$.)

Schwarz lemma. If $f:\mathbb{D}\to\mathbb{D}$ is holomorphic with $f(0)=0$, then $|f(z)|\le |z|$ and $|f'(0)|\le 1$. Equality at any interior $z$ forces $f(z) = e^{i\theta}z$.

5. Laurent series and residues

Drop the assumption that $f$ is holomorphic on the whole disc; allow an isolated singularity at $z_0$. On any annulus $0 < |z - z_0| < R$ on which $f$ is holomorphic, we have a unique Laurent expansion

$$f(z) \;=\; \sum_{n=-\infty}^\infty c_n (z-z_0)^n.$$

The coefficients are still given by Cauchy's formula:

$$c_n = \frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_{|z-z_0|=r}\frac{f(z)}{(z-z_0)^{n+1}}\,dz.$$

Singularities are classified by which negative coefficients appear:

removable all $c_{n}=0$ for $n<0$ — $f$ extends holomorphically across $z_0$ (Riemann's removable-singularity theorem: this happens iff $f$ is bounded near $z_0$).

pole of order $k$ $c_{-k}\ne 0$ but $c_n=0$ for $n<-k$ — $f$ blows up like $1/(z-z_0)^k$.

essential infinitely many $c_n\ne 0$ for $n<0$. Casorati–Weierstrass: $f$ comes arbitrarily close to every complex value in any neighbourhood of $z_0$. Picard sharpens this dramatically: $f$ hits every value (with at most one exception), infinitely often.

The residue of $f$ at $z_0$ is

$$\operatorname{Res}(f,z_0) \;=\; c_{-1} \;=\; \frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint f\,dz.$$

For a simple pole $\operatorname{Res}(f,z_0) = \lim_{z\to z_0}(z-z_0)f(z)$. For a pole of order $k$, $\operatorname{Res}(f,z_0) = \dfrac{1}{(k-1)!}\lim_{z\to z_0}\dfrac{d^{k-1}}{dz^{k-1}}\bigl((z-z_0)^k f(z)\bigr)$.

Residue theorem. Let $\gamma$ be a positively oriented simple closed contour bounding a region $D$, with $f$ holomorphic on a neighbourhood of $\overline D$ except for finitely many isolated singularities $z_k\in D$. Then $$\oint_\gamma f\,dz \;=\; 2\pi i\sum_k \operatorname{Res}(f, z_k).$$

Real definite integrals fall to this, almost mechanically. Famous example: closing $\int_{-\infty}^\infty\frac{dx}{x^2+1}$ in the upper half-plane with a giant semicircle. The semicircular piece tends to $0$ (the integrand decays), the only pole inside is $z=i$ with residue $1/(2i)$, and so the integral is $2\pi i\cdot 1/(2i) = \pi$. Replace the integrand by $e^{ix}/(x^2+1)$ and the answer is $\pi/e$.

Try it: the contour widget in §3 above already computes $\oint f\,dz$ as $2\pi i \sum\mathrm{Res}$ for the enclosed poles. Drag a vertex over a pole to see the sum jump.

6. Argument principle and Rouché

Apply the residue theorem to the logarithmic derivative $f'/f$. If $f$ is meromorphic with zeros $a_j$ of order $m_j$ and poles $b_k$ of order $n_k$, then $f'/f$ has simple poles at each $a_j$ with residue $m_j$, and at each $b_k$ with residue $-n_k$. Hence

$$\frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint_\gamma \frac{f'(z)}{f(z)}\,dz \;=\; Z - P,$$

where $Z = \sum m_j$ counts zeros enclosed by $\gamma$ (with multiplicity) and $P = \sum n_k$ counts poles. This is the argument principle.

The geometric meaning: the integrand is $d(\log f) = d(\log|f|) + i\,d(\arg f)$. The real part integrates to $0$ around a closed loop (it is exact). The imaginary part integrates to $2\pi$ times the winding number of the image curve $f\circ\gamma$ around $0$. So $Z - P$ is exactly how many times the image of $\gamma$ winds around the origin.

Rouché's theorem. If $|f(z) - g(z)| < |f(z)|$ on a contour $\gamma$, then $f$ and $g$ have the same number of zeros inside. Picture: the image $g(\gamma)$ is "less than $|f(\gamma)|$ away" from $f(\gamma)$, so it cannot loop around $0$ a different number of times.

7. Conformal maps

A holomorphic map $f$ is conformal wherever $f'\ne 0$. Globally, a conformal isomorphism is a holomorphic bijection with holomorphic inverse. The principle theorem is overwhelming:

Riemann mapping theorem. Every simply connected proper open subset of $\mathbb{C}$ is conformally equivalent to the open unit disk $\mathbb{D}$. (And the equivalence is unique once you fix a base point and a tangent direction.)

Topology says the disk and any other simply connected domain are homeomorphic; the Riemann mapping theorem says they are holomorphically equivalent. The proof is non-constructive — the equivalence is the unique normalized solution to an extremal problem (maximize $|f'(z_0)|$ among holomorphic injections into $\mathbb{D}$).

Automorphisms

The conformal automorphisms of the disk are exactly

$$\mathrm{Aut}(\mathbb{D}) = \left\{ z\mapsto e^{i\theta}\frac{z-a}{1-\bar a z} \;:\; \theta\in\mathbb{R},\, a\in\mathbb{D}\right\} \;\cong\; \mathrm{PSU}(1,1).$$

This is the isometry group of the hyperbolic plane, with the Poincaré metric $ds^2 = \dfrac{4|dz|^2}{(1-|z|^2)^2}$.

The Cayley transform $z\mapsto i\dfrac{1-z}{1+z}$ identifies the disk with the upper half-plane $\mathbb{H} = \{z : \mathrm{Im}\,z > 0\}$, whose automorphism group is $\mathrm{PSL}_2(\mathbb{R})$ acting by Möbius transformations with real coefficients.

Joukowski and Schwarz–Christoffel

The Joukowski map $J(z) = \tfrac12(z + 1/z)$ has the celebrated property that it sends a circle through $z=1$ (or $z=-1$) to an "airfoil" shape — historically used in early 20th-century aerodynamics to compute flow around wings.

Schwarz–Christoffel maps express the Riemann map from the upper half-plane to a polygon explicitly:

$$f(z) = \int_0^z \prod_{k=1}^n (\zeta - x_k)^{\alpha_k - 1}\,d\zeta,$$

where $x_k\in\mathbb{R}$ are the pre-images of the polygon vertices and $\pi\alpha_k$ are the interior angles at those vertices.

8. Coda — harmonic functions, continuation, monodromy

If $f = u + iv$ is holomorphic, both $u$ and $v$ are harmonic: they satisfy Laplace's equation $\Delta u = u_{xx} + u_{yy} = 0$. Conversely, any harmonic function on a simply connected domain is the real part of some holomorphic $f$, unique up to a real additive constant. The imaginary part $v$ is the harmonic conjugate of $u$; you reconstruct it by integrating the Cauchy–Riemann equations.

This identification is the bridge to potential theory: the steady-state heat distribution, the gravitational potential in vacuum, and the electrostatic potential in a charge-free region are all harmonic, hence locally real parts of holomorphic functions. Conformal maps then move boundary value problems between domains.

Analytic continuation. A holomorphic function on a small disk has a unique extension to any simply connected domain containing the disk on which the extension exists. (Identity theorem: two holomorphic functions agreeing on a set with an accumulation point agree everywhere on the connected component.) The natural domain of $\sqrt{z}$ or $\log z$ is not $\mathbb{C}\setminus\{0\}$ but a bigger object — a Riemann surface. Walking $\log z$ around a loop encircling $0$ adds $2\pi i$; that increment is the monodromy.

Riemann surfaces are the natural homes of multi-valued analytic functions. The compact ones are classified by genus; the simply connected ones by uniformization (every compact Riemann surface of genus $\ge 2$ is a quotient of the upper half-plane by a Fuchsian group). Modular forms — automorphic functions on the upper half-plane — pick up here. Forward pointer: a future modular-forms.html will continue this thread.

Holomorphic = locally rotation+scaling = locally a power series = $u,v$ harmonic conjugates = closed contour integrals vanish = boundary values determine interior. Six perspectives on one phenomenon.

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = $('#mob-svg');
  const ro = $('#mob-readout');
  if(!svg||!ro) return;
  const W=760,H=360,L={cx:190,cy:180,sc:130}, R={cx:570,cy:180,sc:130};
  let useSphere = false;

  // Three source points (z1,z2,z3) and target (w1,w2,w3) determine T.
  // We'll use presets that fix targets and pick source triple as (1, i, -1).
  const presets = {
    id:      {a:[1,0],b:[0,0],c:[0,0],d:[1,0]},
    rot:     {a:[Math.cos(Math.PI/3),Math.sin(Math.PI/3)],b:[0,0],c:[0,0],d:[1,0]},
    cayley:  {a:[1,0],b:[0,-1],c:[1,0],d:[0,1]}, // (z-i)/(z+i)
    inv:     {a:[0,0],b:[1,0],c:[1,0],d:[0,0]},  // 1/z
    elliptic:{a:[Math.cos(Math.PI/4),Math.sin(Math.PI/4)],b:[0,0],c:[0,0],d:[Math.cos(-Math.PI/4),Math.sin(-Math.PI/4)]},
  };
  let M = presets.rot;

  function T(z){
    // T(z) = (a z + b) / (c z + d)
    const num = C.add(C.mul(M.a,z), M.b);
    const den = C.add(C.mul(M.c,z), M.d);
    if(C.abs(den)<1e-9) return [1e6,1e6];
    return C.div(num,den);
  }
  function toLeftPx(z){ return [L.cx + z[0]*L.sc, L.cy - z[1]*L.sc]; }
  function toRightPx(z){ return [R.cx + z[0]*R.sc, R.cy - z[1]*R.sc]; }
  // Stereographic from sphere coords back to plane: project (X,Y,Z) on unit sphere
  // to z = (X+iY)/(1-Z). Inverse: z=x+iy ↦ ( 2x/(1+|z|²), 2y/(1+|z|²), (|z|²-1)/(|z|²+1) )
  function planeToSphereDisk(z){
    // Plot (X,Y) coords as if viewed from above; clamp by |z|
    const r2 = z[0]*z[0]+z[1]*z[1];
    const X = 2*z[0]/(1+r2), Y = 2*z[1]/(1+r2);
    return [X, Y];
  }

  function clearAndDraw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // panels
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:30,y:30,width:320,height:300,fill:'#0b0f16',stroke:'var(--line)','rx':6}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:410,y:30,width:320,height:300,fill:'#0b0f16',stroke:'var(--line)','rx':6}));
    const lt = SVG('text',{x:190,y:50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'}); lt.textContent='source ' + (useSphere?'(sphere view)':'(plane)');
    const rt = SVG('text',{x:570,y:50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'}); rt.textContent='target T(·)';
    svg.appendChild(lt); svg.appendChild(rt);
    // axes
    if(!useSphere){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:30,y1:L.cy,x2:350,y2:L.cy,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L.cx,y1:30,x2:L.cx,y2:330,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    } else {
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:L.cx,cy:L.cy,r:L.sc,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--line)'}));
      // latitude lines
      for(let k=1;k<=2;k++){
        svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:L.cx,cy:L.cy,r:L.sc*k/3,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-dasharray':'2 4'}));
      }
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:410,y1:R.cy,x2:730,y2:R.cy,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:R.cx,y1:30,x2:R.cx,y2:330,stroke:'var(--line)'}));

    // Draw a lattice of points & their images
    const pts = [];
    for(let ix=-2;ix<=2;ix+=0.5)for(let iy=-2;iy<=2;iy+=0.5){
      pts.push([ix,iy]);
    }
    pts.forEach(z=>{
      const wL = useSphere ? (function(){
        const xy = planeToSphereDisk(z);
        return [L.cx + xy[0]*L.sc, L.cy - xy[1]*L.sc];
      })() : toLeftPx(z);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:wL[0],cy:wL[1],r:1.5,fill:'var(--mute)'}));
      const tw = T(z);
      if(Math.abs(tw[0])<10 && Math.abs(tw[1])<10){
        const wR = toRightPx(tw);
        svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:wR[0],cy:wR[1],r:1.5,fill:'var(--cyan)'}));
      }
    });
    // unit circle pre & post
    const n=64;
    const lp=[], rp=[];
    for(let k=0;k<=n;k++){
      const t = 2*Math.PI*k/n;
      const z = [Math.cos(t),Math.sin(t)];
      const w = T(z);
      const lpx = useSphere ? (function(){const xy=planeToSphereDisk(z); return [L.cx+xy[0]*L.sc, L.cy-xy[1]*L.sc];})() : toLeftPx(z);
      lp.push(lpx);
      if(Math.abs(w[0])<10 && Math.abs(w[1])<10) rp.push(toRightPx(w)); else rp.push(null);
    }
    let pathL = 'M'+lp[0].join(' '); for(let k=1;k<lp.length;k++) pathL += ' L'+lp[k].join(' ');
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:pathL, stroke:'var(--yellow)', fill:'none','stroke-width':1.5}));
    let pathR = ''; let started = false;
    for(let k=0;k<rp.length;k++){
      if(!rp[k]){ started=false; continue; }
      pathR += (started?' L':'M') + rp[k].join(' ');
      started = true;
    }
    if(pathR) svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:pathR, stroke:'var(--yellow)', fill:'none','stroke-width':1.5}));

    // Three marker points
    const markers = [['1',[1,0],'var(--green)'],['i',[0,1],'var(--pink)'],['−1',[-1,0],'var(--violet)']];
    markers.forEach(([lab,z,c])=>{
      const lpx = useSphere ? (function(){const xy=planeToSphereDisk(z); return [L.cx+xy[0]*L.sc, L.cy-xy[1]*L.sc];})() : toLeftPx(z);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:lpx[0],cy:lpx[1],r:5,fill:c}));
      const lt = SVG('text',{x:lpx[0]+8,y:lpx[1]-4,'font-size':12,fill:c}); lt.textContent=lab; svg.appendChild(lt);
      const w = T(z);
      if(Math.abs(w[0])<10 && Math.abs(w[1])<10){
        const rpx = toRightPx(w);
        svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:rpx[0],cy:rpx[1],r:5,fill:c}));
      }
    });

    // readout
    const det = C.sub(C.mul(M.a,M.d), C.mul(M.b,M.c));
    ro.textContent = `T(z) = (${C.fmt(M.a)} · z + ${C.fmt(M.b)}) / (${C.fmt(M.c)} · z + ${C.fmt(M.d)})\n`+
      `det = ad − bc = ${C.fmt(det)}\n`+
      `T(1) = ${C.fmt(T([1,0]))}, T(i) = ${C.fmt(T([0,1]))}, T(−1) = ${C.fmt(T([-1,0]))}`;
  }
  $('#mob-preset').addEventListener('change', e=>{ M = presets[e.target.value]; clearAndDraw(); });
  $('#mob-sphere').addEventListener('change', e=>{ useSphere = e.target.checked; clearAndDraw(); });
  clearAndDraw();
})();
</script>

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = $('#dc-svg');
  const sel = $('#dc-fn');
  const rad = $('#dc-radius');
  const rlab = $('#dc-rlabel');
  if(!svg) return;
  const N = 80;
  const SIZE = 480;
  const cell = SIZE / N;
  // Pre-create cells once
  const rects = [];
  // background
  svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:0,y:0,width:SIZE,height:SIZE,fill:'#000'}));
  for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
    for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
      const r = SVG('rect',{x:i*cell, y:j*cell, width:cell+0.5, height:cell+0.5, fill:'#000'});
      svg.appendChild(r);
      rects.push(r);
    }
  }
  // axes
  svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:0,y1:SIZE/2,x2:SIZE,y2:SIZE/2,stroke:'rgba(255,255,255,0.18)'}));
  svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:SIZE/2,y1:0,x2:SIZE/2,y2:SIZE,stroke:'rgba(255,255,255,0.18)'}));

  const fns = {
    z:    z=>z,
    z2:   z=>C.mul(z,z),
    z3:   z=>C.mul(C.mul(z,z),z),
    inv:  z=>{const m=z[0]*z[0]+z[1]*z[1]; if(m<1e-12) return [1e6,0]; return [z[0]/m,-z[1]/m];},
    exp:  z=>C.exp(z),
    sin:  z=>C.sin(z),
    cos:  z=>C.cos(z),
    log:  z=>{const m=Math.hypot(z[0],z[1]); if(m<1e-9) return [-20,0]; return [Math.log(m), Math.atan2(z[1],z[0])];},
    rat1: z=>{const a=C.sub(C.mul(z,z),[1,0]); const b=C.add(C.mul(z,z),[1,0]); return C.div(a,b);},
    cubic:z=>C.sub(C.mul(C.mul(z,z),z), z),
  };

  function hsl(h, s, l){
    // h in [0,360), s,l in [0,1] → "hsl(...)"
    return `hsl(${h.toFixed(0)},${(s*100).toFixed(0)}%,${(l*100).toFixed(0)}%)`;
  }
  function colorFor(w){
    if(!isFinite(w[0])||!isFinite(w[1])) return '#000';
    const m = Math.hypot(w[0],w[1]);
    let h = (Math.atan2(w[1],w[0]) * 180 / Math.PI + 360) % 360;
    // lightness from log|f|, with bands
    const lg = Math.log2(m + 1e-12);
    const frac = lg - Math.floor(lg); // [0,1)
    // Make light increase with frac, with periodic bands
    const l = 0.18 + 0.62 * frac;
    return hsl(h, 0.85, l);
  }

  function recolor(){
    const R = parseFloat(rad.value);
    rlab.textContent = R.toFixed(1);
    const f = fns[sel.value];
    let idx = 0;
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
        const x = -R + (i+0.5) * (2*R/N);
        const y =  R - (j+0.5) * (2*R/N);
        const w = f([x,y]);
        rects[idx++].setAttribute('fill', colorFor(w));
      }
    }
  }
  sel.addEventListener('change', recolor);
  rad.addEventListener('input', recolor);
  recolor();
})();
</script>

(function(){
  const svg = $('#ct-svg');
  const ro = $('#ct-readout');
  if(!svg||!ro) return;
  const W=760, H=460;
  const cx=W/2, cy=H/2, sc=80;
  const toPx = z => [cx + z[0]*sc, cy - z[1]*sc];
  const toZ  = (x,y) => [(x-cx)/sc, -(y-cy)/sc];

  const fns = {
    r1:{ name:'1/(z²+1)',
         poles:[[0,1],[0,-1]],
         res:p=>{ // 1/((z-i)(z+i)) — residue at z=i is 1/(2i), at z=-i is -1/(2i)
           if(Math.abs(p[1]-1)<1e-6) return [0,-0.5];
           if(Math.abs(p[1]+1)<1e-6) return [0,0.5];
           return [0,0];
         },
         f:z=>C.div([1,0], C.add(C.mul(z,z),[1,0])) },
    r2:{ name:'eⁱᶻ/(z²+1)',
         poles:[[0,1],[0,-1]],
         res:p=>{
           // residue at z=i: e^{i·i}/(2i) = e^{-1}/(2i) = -i·e^{-1}/2
           if(Math.abs(p[1]-1)<1e-6) return [0, -Math.exp(-1)/2];
           // residue at z=-i: e^{i·(-i)}/(-2i) = e^{1}/(-2i) = i·e/2
           if(Math.abs(p[1]+1)<1e-6) return [0,  Math.exp(1)/2];
           return [0,0];
         },
         f:z=>C.div(C.exp([(-z[1]),z[0]]), C.add(C.mul(z,z),[1,0])) },
    r3:{ name:'1/(z(z−1)(z+2))',
         poles:[[0,0],[1,0],[-2,0]],
         res:p=>{
           if(Math.abs(p[0])<1e-6 && Math.abs(p[1])<1e-6) return [-0.5, 0]; // 1/((-1)(2)) = -1/2
           if(Math.abs(p[0]-1)<1e-6) return [1/3, 0];                       // 1/(1·3) = 1/3
           if(Math.abs(p[0]+2)<1e-6) return [1/6, 0];                       // 1/((-2)(-3)) = 1/6
           return [0,0];
         },
         f:z=>C.div([1,0], C.mul(C.mul(z, C.sub(z,[1,0])), C.add(z,[2,0]))) },
    r4:{ name:'z/(z²−1)',
         poles:[[1,0],[-1,0]],
         res:p=>{
           if(Math.abs(p[0]-1)<1e-6) return [0.5,0];
           if(Math.abs(p[0]+1)<1e-6) return [0.5,0];
           return [0,0];
         },
         f:z=>C.div(z, C.sub(C.mul(z,z),[1,0])) },
    r5:{ name:'1/(z²+2z+2)',
         poles:[[-1,1],[-1,-1]],
         res:p=>{
           if(Math.abs(p[0]+1)<1e-6 && Math.abs(p[1]-1)<1e-6) return [0,-0.5];
           if(Math.abs(p[0]+1)<1e-6 && Math.abs(p[1]+1)<1e-6) return [0,0.5];
           return [0,0];
         },
         f:z=>C.div([1,0], C.add(C.add(C.mul(z,z), C.scale(z,2)),[2,0])) },
  };
  let curr = 'r1';
  let verts = []; // in plane coords
  const presets = {
    big: ()=>{const v=[]; for(let k=0;k<8;k++){const t=2*Math.PI*k/8; v.push([2.6*Math.cos(t),2.6*Math.sin(t)]);} return v;},
    rect:()=>[[-2.2,-1.4],[2.2,-1.4],[2.2,1.4],[-2.2,1.4]],
    tri: ()=>[[-2.2,-1.2],[2.5,-1.2],[0.2,2.0]],
    upper: ()=>{ const v=[[-2.6,0]]; for(let k=0;k<=10;k++){const t=Math.PI*k/10; v.push([2.6*Math.cos(t), 2.6*Math.sin(t)]);} v.push([-2.6,0]); return v; },
  };
  function loadShape(name){ verts = presets[name](); draw(); }

  function pointInPoly(p, poly){
    let inside = false;
    for(let i=0,j=poly.length-1;i<poly.length;j=i++){
      const xi=poly[i][0], yi=poly[i][1], xj=poly[j][0], yj=poly[j][1];
      const intersect = ((yi>p[1])!=(yj>p[1])) && (p[0] < (xj-xi)*(p[1]-yi)/((yj-yi)||1e-12) + xi);
      if(intersect) inside = !inside;
    }
    return inside;
  }
  function polyOrientation(poly){
    let s = 0;
    for(let i=0;i<poly.length;i++){
      const a = poly[i], b = poly[(i+1)%poly.length];
      s += (b[0]-a[0])*(b[1]+a[1]);
    }
    return s < 0 ? 1 : -1; // positive (CCW) when shoelace sum < 0 (because of sign convention)
  }

  let drag = -1;
  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:20,y:20,width:W-40,height:H-40,fill:'#0b0f16',stroke:'var(--line)',rx:6}));
    // grid
    for(let g=-4;g<=4;g++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:cx+g*sc,y1:30,x2:cx+g*sc,y2:H-30,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-dasharray':'1 3'}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:30,y1:cy+g*sc,x2:W-30,y2:cy+g*sc,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-dasharray':'1 3'}));
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:30,y1:cy,x2:W-30,y2:cy,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:cx,y1:30,x2:cx,y2:H-30,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));

    // poles
    const F = fns[curr];
    F.poles.forEach(p=>{
      const px = toPx(p);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px[0],cy:px[1],r:6,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:px[0]+10,y:px[1]-6,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--pink)'})).textContent='×';
    });

    // contour
    if(verts.length>1){
      let d = 'M' + toPx(verts[0]).join(' ');
      for(let k=1;k<verts.length;k++){
        d += ' L' + toPx(verts[k]).join(' ');
      }
      d += ' Z';
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d, stroke:'var(--yellow)', fill:'rgba(255,216,102,0.06)','stroke-width':2}));
      // direction arrows on edges
      for(let k=0;k<verts.length;k++){
        const a = verts[k], b = verts[(k+1)%verts.length];
        const m = [(a[0]+b[0])/2, (a[1]+b[1])/2];
        const dr = [b[0]-a[0], b[1]-a[1]];
        const L = Math.hypot(dr[0],dr[1])||1;
        const tip = [m[0]+0.08*dr[0]/L*Math.min(L*sc,30)/sc, m[1]+0.08*dr[1]/L*Math.min(L*sc,30)/sc];
        drawArrow(svg, toPx(m), toPx(tip), {color:'var(--yellow)', markerId:'ct-dir', width:0});
      }
    }
    // vertex handles
    verts.forEach((v,i)=>{
      const px = toPx(v);
      const c = SVG('circle',{cx:px[0],cy:px[1],r:5,fill:'var(--yellow)','stroke':'#222','stroke-width':1,'data-i':i,'class':'ct-vert','style':'cursor:grab'});
      svg.appendChild(c);
    });

    // residue sum
    const orient = polyOrientation(verts);
    let sum = [0,0];
    let enc = [];
    F.poles.forEach(p=>{
      if(pointInPoly(p, verts)){
        const r = F.res(p);
        sum = C.add(sum, r);
        enc.push(p);
      }
    });
    const integral = C.scale(C.mul([0, 2*Math.PI], sum), orient);
    let txt = `f(z) = ${F.name}\nenclosed poles: ` + (enc.length? enc.map(p=>'('+p[0].toFixed(2)+(p[1]>=0?'+':'')+p[1].toFixed(2)+'i)').join(', ') : 'none') + '\n';
    enc.forEach(p=>{ txt += `  Res(f, ${p[0].toFixed(2)}${p[1]>=0?'+':''}${p[1].toFixed(2)}i) = ${C.fmt(F.res(p))}\n`; });
    txt += `orientation: ${orient>0?'counterclockwise (+)':'clockwise (−)'}\n`;
    txt += `∮ f dz = 2πi · (sum of residues) · orient = ${C.fmt(integral)}`;
    ro.textContent = txt;
  }

  svg.addEventListener('mousedown', e=>{
    if(e.target.classList.contains('ct-vert')){
      drag = parseInt(e.target.getAttribute('data-i'));
    }
  });
  window.addEventListener('mousemove', e=>{
    if(drag<0) return;
    const r = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = (e.clientX - r.left) * (W/r.width);
    const y = (e.clientY - r.top) * (H/r.height);
    verts[drag] = toZ(x,y);
    draw();
  });
  window.addEventListener('mouseup', ()=>{ drag = -1; });

  $('#ct-fn').addEventListener('change', e=>{ curr = e.target.value; draw(); });
  $('#ct-shape').addEventListener('change', e=>loadShape(e.target.value));
  $('#ct-reset').addEventListener('click', ()=>loadShape($('#ct-shape').value));
  loadShape('rect');
})();
</script>

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = $('#tay-svg');
  const ro = $('#tay-readout');
  if(!svg||!ro) return;
  const SIZE = 480;
  const cx = SIZE/2, cy = SIZE/2, sc = 90;
  const toPx = z => [cx + z[0]*sc, cy - z[1]*sc];
  const toZ  = (x,y) => [(x-cx)/sc, -(y-cy)/sc];

  const fns = {
    geom:  {name:'1/(1−z)', sing:[[1,0]],
            coeffs:(z0,N)=>{ // 1/(1-z) about z0: 1/((1-z0)-(z-z0)) = sum (z-z0)^n / (1-z0)^{n+1}
              const w = [1-z0[0], -z0[1]];
              const out=[]; let cur=[1,0];
              for(let n=0;n<N;n++){ out.push(C.div(cur, C.mul(w, [1,0]))); cur = C.div(cur, w); }
              // Actually coeff_n = 1/(1-z0)^{n+1}
              const coefs=[]; let p=[1,0];
              for(let n=0;n<N;n++){ p = C.div(p, w); coefs.push(p); }
              return coefs;
            }},
    invq:  {name:'1/(z²+1)', sing:[[0,1],[0,-1]], coeffs:null},
    log1z: {name:'log(1+z)', sing:[[-1,0]], coeffs:null},
    tan:   {name:'tan z', sing:(()=>{ const s=[]; for(let k=-3;k<=3;k++) s.push([Math.PI*(k+0.5),0]); return s; })(), coeffs:null},
  };
  let z0 = [0,0];
  let curr = 'geom';

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:0,y:0,width:SIZE,height:SIZE,fill:'#0b0f16',stroke:'var(--line)',rx:6}));
    // grid
    for(let g=-3;g<=3;g++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:cx+g*sc,y1:0,x2:cx+g*sc,y2:SIZE,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-dasharray':'1 3'}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:0,y1:cy+g*sc,x2:SIZE,y2:cy+g*sc,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-dasharray':'1 3'}));
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:0,y1:cy,x2:SIZE,y2:cy,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:cx,y1:0,x2:cx,y2:SIZE,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    // singularities
    const F = fns[curr];
    F.sing.forEach(s=>{
      const px = toPx(s);
      if(px[0]<0||px[0]>SIZE||px[1]<0||px[1]>SIZE) return;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px[0],cy:px[1],r:7,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':2}));
      const xt = SVG('text',{x:px[0]+10,y:px[1]-6,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--pink)'}); xt.textContent='sing'; svg.appendChild(xt);
    });
    // distance to nearest singularity
    let R = Infinity, nearest=null;
    F.sing.forEach(s=>{ const d = Math.hypot(z0[0]-s[0], z0[1]-s[1]); if(d<R){ R=d; nearest=s; } });
    // disk of convergence
    const z0p = toPx(z0);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:z0p[0],cy:z0p[1],r:R*sc,fill:'rgba(131,193,103,0.12)',stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':2}));
    // line from z0 to nearest sing
    if(nearest){
      const np = toPx(nearest);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:z0p[0],y1:z0p[1],x2:np[0],y2:np[1],stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-dasharray':'4 4'}));
    }
    // z0 marker
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:z0p[0],cy:z0p[1],r:5,fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    const lt = SVG('text',{x:z0p[0]+8,y:z0p[1]-6,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'}); lt.textContent='z₀'; svg.appendChild(lt);
    // readout
    let txt = `f(z) = ${F.name}\nz₀ = ${C.fmt(z0)}\nradius of convergence R = distance(z₀, nearest sing) = ${R.toFixed(4)}\nnearest singularity at ${nearest? C.fmt(nearest):'∞'}\n`;
    if(curr==='geom'){
      // 1/(1-z) coefficients about z0: c_n = 1/(1-z0)^{n+1}
      const w = [1-z0[0], -z0[1]];
      let p=[1,0]; const out=[];
      for(let n=0;n<5;n++){ p = C.div([1,0], (function(){let r=[1,0]; for(let k=0;k<=n;k++) r=C.mul(r,w); return r;})());
        out.push(`c_${n} = ${C.fmt(p,4)}`); }
      txt += '\nfirst Taylor coefficients about z₀:\n  ' + out.join('\n  ');
    }
    ro.textContent = txt;
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', e=>{
    const r = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = (e.clientX - r.left) * (SIZE/r.width);
    const y = (e.clientY - r.top) * (SIZE/r.height);
    z0 = toZ(x,y);
    draw();
  });
  $('#tay-fn').addEventListener('change', e=>{ curr = e.target.value; draw(); });
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = $('#ag-svg');
  const ro = $('#ag-readout');
  if(!svg||!ro) return;
  const W=760,H=380;
  const L={cx:190,cy:190,sc:80}, R={cx:570,cy:190,sc:30};
  const toLpx = z => [L.cx + z[0]*L.sc, L.cy - z[1]*L.sc];
  const toRpx = z => [R.cx + z[0]*R.sc, R.cy - z[1]*R.sc];

  const fns = {
    z:    {name:'z',                f:z=>z,                                                   zeros:[[0,0]], poles:[]},
    z2:   {name:'z²',               f:z=>C.mul(z,z),                                          zeros:[[0,0]], poles:[]},
    z3:   {name:'z³',               f:z=>C.mul(C.mul(z,z),z),                                 zeros:[[0,0]], poles:[]},
    zm1:  {name:'z²−1',             f:z=>C.sub(C.mul(z,z),[1,0]),                             zeros:[[1,0],[-1,0]], poles:[]},
    cube3:{name:'(z−1)(z−i)(z+1)',  f:z=>C.mul(C.mul(C.sub(z,[1,0]),C.sub(z,[0,1])),C.add(z,[1,0])), zeros:[[1,0],[0,1],[-1,0]], poles:[]},
    rat:  {name:'(z−0.3)/(z+0.6i)', f:z=>C.div(C.sub(z,[0.3,0]), C.add(z,[0,0.6])),           zeros:[[0.3,0]], poles:[[0,-0.6]]},
  };
  let curr = 'z2';

  function pointInDisk(p, c, r){ return Math.hypot(p[0]-c[0], p[1]-c[1]) < r; }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:30,y:30,width:320,height:320,fill:'#0b0f16',stroke:'var(--line)',rx:6}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:410,y:30,width:320,height:320,fill:'#0b0f16',stroke:'var(--line)',rx:6}));
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:30,y1:L.cy,x2:350,y2:L.cy,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L.cx,y1:30,x2:L.cx,y2:350,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:410,y1:R.cy,x2:730,y2:R.cy,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:R.cx,y1:30,x2:R.cx,y2:350,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:190,y:50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'})).textContent='source plane (γ)';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:570,y:50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'})).textContent='image f(γ)';

    const F = fns[curr];
    const cx = parseFloat($('#ag-cx').value), cy = parseFloat($('#ag-cy').value), Rad = parseFloat($('#ag-r').value);
    $('#ag-rlabel').textContent = Rad.toFixed(2);

    // zeros & poles
    F.zeros.forEach(z=>{ const px = toLpx(z); svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px[0],cy:px[1],r:5,fill:'var(--green)'})); });
    F.poles.forEach(z=>{ const px = toLpx(z); svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px[0],cy:px[1],r:5,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':2})); });

    // contour: circle of radius Rad around (cx,cy)
    const N = 200;
    const path = [];
    const imgPath = [];
    for(let k=0;k<=N;k++){
      const t = 2*Math.PI*k/N;
      const z = [cx + Rad*Math.cos(t), cy + Rad*Math.sin(t)];
      path.push(toLpx(z));
      const w = F.f(z);
      imgPath.push(toRpx(w));
    }
    let dL = 'M' + path[0].join(' '); for(let k=1;k<path.length;k++) dL += ' L' + path[k].join(' ');
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dL, stroke:'var(--yellow)', fill:'none','stroke-width':2}));
    // Image path with rainbow fade
    for(let k=0;k<imgPath.length-1;k++){
      const hue = (360*k/imgPath.length).toFixed(0);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:imgPath[k][0],y1:imgPath[k][1],x2:imgPath[k+1][0],y2:imgPath[k+1][1], stroke:`hsl(${hue},70%,60%)`,'stroke-width':1.7}));
    }
    // origin marker on right
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:R.cx,cy:R.cy,r:4,fill:'var(--violet)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:R.cx+8,y:R.cy+4,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--violet)'})).textContent='0';

    // winding number: integrate d(arg) along image path
    let wind = 0;
    for(let k=0;k<imgPath.length-1;k++){
      const ax = imgPath[k][0]-R.cx, ay = R.cy-imgPath[k][1];
      const bx = imgPath[k+1][0]-R.cx, by = R.cy-imgPath[k+1][1];
      let da = Math.atan2(by, bx) - Math.atan2(ay, ax);
      if(da > Math.PI) da -= 2*Math.PI;
      if(da < -Math.PI) da += 2*Math.PI;
      wind += da;
    }
    wind = Math.round(wind / (2*Math.PI));

    // count zeros and poles enclosed
    let Z=0, P=0;
    F.zeros.forEach(z=>{ if(pointInDisk(z,[cx,cy],Rad)) Z++; });
    F.poles.forEach(z=>{ if(pointInDisk(z,[cx,cy],Rad)) P++; });

    ro.textContent = `f(z) = ${F.name}\ncontour: |z − (${cx.toFixed(2)}+${cy.toFixed(2)}i)| = ${Rad.toFixed(2)}\nenclosed zeros Z = ${Z}, enclosed poles P = ${P}\nwinding number of f(γ) around 0 = ${wind}\nargument principle: Z − P = ${Z-P}  ${wind===Z-P? '✓' : '(adjust contour to avoid passing through a zero/pole)'}`;
  }
  ['ag-fn','ag-r','ag-cx','ag-cy'].forEach(id=>{
    const el = $('#'+id);
    el.addEventListener('input', ()=>{ if(id==='ag-fn') curr = el.value; draw(); });
    el.addEventListener('change', ()=>{ if(id==='ag-fn') curr = el.value; draw(); });
  });
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = $('#cf-svg');
  const ro = $('#cf-readout');
  if(!svg||!ro) return;
  const W=760, H=380;
  const L={cx:190,cy:190,sc:60}, R={cx:570,cy:190,sc:60};
  const toLpx = z => [L.cx + z[0]*L.sc, L.cy - z[1]*L.sc];
  const toRpx = z => [R.cx + z[0]*R.sc, R.cy - z[1]*R.sc];

  const maps = {
    z2: {
      name:'z ↦ z²',
      f: z => C.mul(z,z),
      gridSrc: 'cart',  // cartesian grid in source
      domain:'plane',
      info:'Conformal except at z=0 (where f′=0). Wrap angles double around the origin: a sector of angle θ becomes a sector of angle 2θ.'
    },
    exp: {
      name:'z ↦ eᶻ',
      f: z => C.exp(z),
      gridSrc:'strip',
      domain:'strip',
      info:'Sends horizontal lines to rays from origin, vertical lines to circles. The strip 0 < Im z < 2π wraps once around the punctured plane.'
    },
    log: {
      name:'z ↦ log z',
      f: z => C.log(z),
      gridSrc:'punctured',
      domain:'punctured',
      info:'Inverse of exp. Slit plane (negative real axis removed) maps biholomorphically to the strip −π < Im < π.'
    },
    cayley: {
      name:'Cayley i(1−z)/(1+z): UHP→𝔻',
      f: z => {
        // (1-z)/(1+z) then multiply by i: i*(1-z)/(1+z)
        const num = C.sub([1,0],z), den = C.add([1,0],z);
        const q = C.div(num,den);
        return C.mul([0,1],q);
      },
      gridSrc:'uhp',
      domain:'uhp',
      info:'The classical biholomorphism between upper half-plane and unit disk. The real axis maps to the unit circle.'
    },
    moebius: {
      name:'Möbius (z−a)/(1−ā z)',
      f: z => {
        const a = [0.4, 0.2];
        const num = C.sub(z,a);
        const den = C.sub([1,0], C.mul([a[0],-a[1]], z));
        return C.div(num,den);
      },
      gridSrc:'disk',
      domain:'disk',
      info:'Disk automorphism with parameter a = 0.4+0.2i. Sends a to 0; preserves hyperbolic distances.'
    },
    joukowski: {
      name:'Joukowski ½(z + 1/z)',
      f: z => C.scale(C.add(z, C.div([1,0],z)), 0.5),
      gridSrc:'circles',
      domain:'plane',
      info:'Sends the unit circle |z|=1 to the segment [-1,1] (twice). A circle through z=1 with centre offset gives an airfoil shape.'
    },
    sc3: {
      name:'Schwarz–Christoffel (UHP → triangle)',
      // Approximate map sending UHP to equilateral triangle: integral of (z+1)^{-2/3}(z-1)^{-2/3}(z)^{-2/3}? Use simpler: UHP -> isoceles via SC integral, sampled.
      f: z => {
        // For visualization just send (x+iy) to its image under a hand-coded SC integral approximation:
        // The map UHP→equilateral triangle has integrand (1-ζ²)^{-2/3}; we evaluate by a few-step Simpson's rule from 0.
        // For our visualization purposes use a direct piecewise mapping: rotate vertical lines into segments inside a triangle.
        const cap = z[1] < 0 ? [z[0], -z[1]] : z;
        // simple deformation: triangle with vertices at -1, 1, i*(√3/2)*1.4
        const tx = cap[0]; const ty = cap[1]/(1+cap[1]); // squish y in (0,1)
        return [tx*(1-ty*0.6), ty*Math.sqrt(3)/2*1.6];
      },
      gridSrc:'uhp',
      domain:'uhp',
      info:'Schematic: Schwarz–Christoffel integrals send the UHP to a polygon. Real axis → polygon perimeter; pre-vertices map to the corners.'
    },
  };

  let curr = 'z2';
  let probe = [0.7,0.5];

  function srcGridLines(kind){
    const L = [];
    if(kind==='cart'){
      for(let x=-2.5;x<=2.5;x+=0.5){
        const line=[]; for(let y=-2.5;y<=2.5;y+=0.05) line.push([x,y]); L.push({line, color:'var(--blue)'});
      }
      for(let y=-2.5;y<=2.5;y+=0.5){
        const line=[]; for(let x=-2.5;x<=2.5;x+=0.05) line.push([x,y]); L.push({line, color:'var(--cyan)'});
      }
    } else if(kind==='strip'){
      for(let y=0;y<=2*Math.PI;y+=Math.PI/4){
        const line=[]; for(let x=-2.5;x<=2.5;x+=0.05) line.push([x,y-Math.PI]); L.push({line, color:'var(--blue)'});
      }
      for(let x=-2.5;x<=2.5;x+=0.5){
        const line=[]; for(let y=-Math.PI;y<=Math.PI;y+=0.05) line.push([x,y]); L.push({line, color:'var(--cyan)'});
      }
    } else if(kind==='punctured'){
      for(let r=0.3;r<=2.5;r+=0.3){
        const line=[]; for(let t=-Math.PI+0.05;t<=Math.PI-0.05;t+=0.05) line.push([r*Math.cos(t),r*Math.sin(t)]); L.push({line, color:'var(--blue)'});
      }
      for(let k=-5;k<=5;k++){
        const t = k*Math.PI/6;
        if(Math.abs(Math.abs(t)-Math.PI)<0.01) continue;
        const line=[]; for(let r=0.05;r<=2.5;r+=0.05) line.push([r*Math.cos(t),r*Math.sin(t)]); L.push({line, color:'var(--cyan)'});
      }
    } else if(kind==='uhp'){
      for(let y=0.1;y<=2.5;y+=0.4){
        const line=[]; for(let x=-2.5;x<=2.5;x+=0.05) line.push([x,y]); L.push({line, color:'var(--blue)'});
      }
      for(let x=-2.5;x<=2.5;x+=0.5){
        const line=[]; for(let y=0.05;y<=2.5;y+=0.05) line.push([x,y]); L.push({line, color:'var(--cyan)'});
      }
    } else if(kind==='disk'){
      for(let r=0.15;r<=0.95;r+=0.15){
        const line=[]; for(let t=0;t<=2*Math.PI;t+=0.05) line.push([r*Math.cos(t),r*Math.sin(t)]); L.push({line, color:'var(--blue)'});
      }
      for(let k=0;k<12;k++){
        const t = k*Math.PI/6;
        const line=[]; for(let r=0;r<=0.95;r+=0.05) line.push([r*Math.cos(t),r*Math.sin(t)]); L.push({line, color:'var(--cyan)'});
      }
    } else if(kind==='circles'){
      // unit circle and a deformed circle to make airfoil
      for(let R=0.6;R<=1.6;R+=0.2){
        const line=[]; for(let t=0;t<=2*Math.PI;t+=0.04) line.push([R*Math.cos(t)-0.07, R*Math.sin(t)+0.04]); L.push({line, color:'var(--blue)'});
      }
      for(let k=0;k<12;k++){
        const t = k*Math.PI/6;
        const line=[]; for(let r=0.5;r<=1.6;r+=0.05) line.push([r*Math.cos(t)-0.07,r*Math.sin(t)+0.04]); L.push({line, color:'var(--cyan)'});
      }
    }
    return L;
  }

  function clip(px, panel){
    return px[0]>panel.cx-160 && px[0]<panel.cx+160 && px[1]>panel.cy-160 && px[1]<panel.cy+160;
  }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:30,y:30,width:320,height:320,fill:'#0b0f16',stroke:'var(--line)',rx:6}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:410,y:30,width:320,height:320,fill:'#0b0f16',stroke:'var(--line)',rx:6}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:190,y:50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'})).textContent='source';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:570,y:50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'})).textContent='target';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:30,y1:L.cy,x2:350,y2:L.cy,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:L.cx,y1:30,x2:L.cx,y2:350,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:410,y1:R.cy,x2:730,y2:R.cy,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:R.cx,y1:30,x2:R.cx,y2:350,stroke:'var(--line)'}));

    const M = maps[curr];
    const lines = srcGridLines(M.gridSrc);
    lines.forEach(({line, color})=>{
      // source
      let dl = ''; let started = false;
      line.forEach(z=>{
        const px = toLpx(z);
        if(clip(px, L)){ dl += (started?' L':'M') + px.join(' '); started = true; }
        else started = false;
      });
      if(dl) svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dl, stroke:color, fill:'none','stroke-width':1, opacity:0.8}));
      // target
      let dr = ''; started = false;
      line.forEach(z=>{
        const w = M.f(z);
        if(!isFinite(w[0])||!isFinite(w[1])){ started=false; return; }
        const px = toRpx(w);
        if(clip(px, R)){ dr += (started?' L':'M') + px.join(' '); started = true; }
        else started = false;
      });
      if(dr) svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dr, stroke:color, fill:'none','stroke-width':1, opacity:0.8}));
    });
    // probe
    const lp = toLpx(probe);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:lp[0],cy:lp[1],r:6,fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    const w = M.f(probe);
    if(isFinite(w[0])&&isFinite(w[1])){
      const rp = toRpx(w);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:rp[0],cy:rp[1],r:6,fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    }
    ro.textContent = `${M.name}\nprobe z = ${C.fmt(probe)},  f(z) = ${C.fmt(w)}\n${M.info}`;
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', e=>{
    const r = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = (e.clientX - r.left) * (W/r.width);
    const y = (e.clientY - r.top) * (H/r.height);
    if(x<350){
      probe = [(x - L.cx)/L.sc, -(y - L.cy)/L.sc];
      draw();
    }
  });
  $('#cf-pick').addEventListener('change', e=>{ curr = e.target.value; draw(); });
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/\u00a0/g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>
